How much should you tip your dog groomer?

Quick:

Holiday tip: Regular clients typically give an extra full-session tip (~$60) in December. This calculator shows your per-visit tip.

Quick reference — common grooming totals

Grooming cost 15% tip 20% tip ★ 25% tip Total (20%)

How much should you tip a dog groomer?

The standard tip for a dog groomer is 15–20% of the grooming cost, with a $5 minimum for any service. For a $60 full groom, that's $9–12. For a $100 groom on a large breed, $15–20 is appropriate.

Dog grooming is physically demanding work — groomers handle animals that are often anxious, squirmy, or reactive, and they spend 1–3 hours per dog in conditions that can be loud and stressful. Your tip is a direct acknowledgment of that skill and patience.

For holiday tipping, the industry standard for regular clients is to give an amount equal to one full grooming session in December. If your dog's regular groom costs $70, a $70 holiday tip (on top of your per-visit tip) is a generous and much-appreciated gesture.

Factors that affect how much to tip

  • Dog size: Large and giant breeds take significantly more time, product, and physical effort. A Great Dane groom is a very different job than a Chihuahua trim — the tip should reflect that difference.
  • Dog behavior: Anxious, aggressive, or squirmy dogs require extra patience and skill. Tip 20–25% when your dog makes the groomer's job harder — they earned it.
  • Mobile vs salon: Mobile groomers come to your door with their own equipment and vehicle. The convenience is worth acknowledging with a slightly higher tip — 20–25% vs the 15–20% standard for salon groomers.
  • Coat condition: Severely matted coats require dematting or shaving, which adds significant time. If your dog came in matted, tip closer to 25%.
  • Frequency: If you're a regular client, consistent tipping at every visit builds goodwill. Your groomer will remember your dog's preferences and give them priority booking on busy days.

Dog groomer tipping etiquette

Cash handed directly to your groomer is always preferred. At chain grooming salons (PetSmart, Petco), card tips go through the system — cash ensures your groomer receives the full amount without any processing deductions.

For standalone nail trims, tip $2–5 even if the service only takes 5–10 minutes. Nail trims on anxious dogs are genuinely stressful work, and a small tip is a meaningful acknowledgment.

If you're unhappy with the groom, bring it up with the groomer or salon manager before leaving. Most groomers will fix any issues. Adjust your tip based on the outcome — not tipping at all for a fixable issue that was resolved is poor form.
